title: betula papyracea var. occidentalis
intro: Betula papyracea var. occidentalis, also known as the western paper birch, is a deciduous tree species native to western North America. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of the western paper birch are said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of the western paper birch include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
